---
title: "Track — State-Space Models"
subtitle: "The other door to linear-time memory: continuous state spaces, selectivity, and why Transformers turn out to be SSMs."
---

State-space models reach the same fixed-size, linear-time memory as the foundations spine, but from a
different starting point: a continuous-time system $h'(t)=Ah(t)+Bx(t)$, discretized into a recurrence and
trained as a convolution. This track builds that lineage from scratch — S4, then Mamba's selectivity,
then Mamba-2's duality — and ends by placing the whole family on M7's three dials.

## The dial setting

M7's master recurrence is

$$\mathbf{S}_t=\alpha_t\,\mathbf{S}_{t-1}+\beta_t\,(\text{write}_t)\,\phi(\mathbf{k}_t)^\top,\qquad \mathbf{o}_t\propto\mathbf{S}_t\,\phi(\mathbf{q}_t).$$

This track turns the **gate** $\alpha_t$ — the dial for *what stays* — on the Hebbian (additive) write
$\text{write}_t=\mathbf{v}_t$. The SSM lineage is that one dial swept across its full range:

- **$\alpha_t=1$** — never forget. This is M3 linear attention, the track's starting point.
- **$\alpha_t$ fixed and structured** — a per-channel decay $\bar A$, set once and tuned to remember. This is **S4** (SSM-1).
- **$\alpha_t$ input-dependent, channel-wise** — the memory picks its own decay per token. This is **Mamba / S6** (SSM-2), the same setting the zoo already names *channel-wise* for the delta write.
- **$\alpha_t=a_t$, a scalar** — the input-dependent scalar special case. This is **Mamba-2** (SSM-3), and collapsing to a scalar is exactly what makes the SSM a masked linear attention.

The **write** dial stays Hebbian throughout the SSM lineage. Its cross-axis — the delta write of M4 — is
where SSMs meet Gated DeltaNet; SSM-4 reads both lineages off the same gate.

**Prerequisites.** Foundations only: [M1](../../foundations/m1-associative-memory.ipynb) (associative
memory), [M3](../../foundations/m3-linear-attention.ipynb) (linear attention *is* fast weights),
[M7](../../foundations/m7-three-dials.ipynb) (the three dials); SSM-4 also builds on
[M4](../../foundations/m4-delta-rule.ipynb) (the gate and the gated delta rule). No other track — tracks
are siblings, never a sequence.

**The papers.**

| Date | Paper | Who | What it added |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2020 | [HiPPO](https://arxiv.org/abs/2008.07669) | Gu et al. | a structured $A$ that provably remembers, from optimal polynomial projection |
| 2021 | [S4](https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.00396) | Gu et al. | the structured state space: the recurrence is a convolution, trainable at scale |
| 2022 | [S4D](https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.11893) | Gu et al. | the diagonal simplification the toys actually run |
| 2023 | [Mamba](https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.00752) | Gu & Dao | selectivity: input-dependent parameters, at the cost of the convolution |
| 2024 | [Mamba-2](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.21060) | Dao & Gu | structured state-space duality: the SSM is masked linear attention |

## The modules

| # | Module | The question it answers |
|---|---|---|
| SSM-1 | [The linear state-space model](ssm1-linear-state-space.ipynb) | How does a fixed linear recurrence become a long-range memory you can train in parallel? |
| SSM-2 | [Selectivity (Mamba / S6)](ssm2-selectivity.ipynb) | What breaks when the recurrence cannot choose what to remember? |
| SSM-3 | [Mamba-2 and the duality](ssm3-mamba2-ssd.ipynb) | If the SSM and linear attention are both linear recurrences, are they the same thing? |
| SSM-4 | [The gate, from none to selective](ssm4-gate-spectrum.ipynb) | Linear attention, RetNet, S4, Mamba, Gated DeltaNet: how many gates is that, really? |

**Asides** — [the parallel scan](ssm2-aside-parallel-scan.ipynb) · [hybrid attention–SSM models](ssm4-aside-hybrid-models.ipynb). Optional; read each when the module points you at it.
